skill.name: video-extend
skill.description: Extend or continue an existing video clip on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes to Google Veo 3-1's `extend-video` and `fast/extend-video` endpoints — pick the source video plus a prompt describing what should happen next, and the model produces a clip that continues the original with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity. Use when the user has a short Veo clip and wants it longer, or wants a chained narrative built shot-by-shot from a single seed clip. Triggers on "extend video", "continue video", "longer video", "video extend", "make this clip longer", "Veo extend", "chain video shots", "video continuation", or any explicit ask to take an existing video and add more frames after it.
skill.summary: Extend existing Veo video clips with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity using the RunComfy CLI and Google Veo 3-1.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 78 static analysis findings are false positives. The high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags target YAML metadata and documentation text in SKILL.md lines 5, 9, 82, and 120. The 44 'shell backtick execution' flags all reference CLI command examples inside markdown code blocks (documentation, not executable injection). The 'hidden file' flag at line 140 is a security transparency disclosure about token storage. All 24 hardcoded URLs point to legitimate runcomfy.com and skills.sh documentation. The skill inherently uses external commands (runcomfy CLI) and network access (RunComfy API), which are necessary and legitimate for its purpose. The skill author properly constrains allowed-tools to Bash(runcomfy *) and documents security mitigations. Published with a medium risk warning because external command execution and API network requests are inherent to the skill's function.

content.user_title: Extend and continue video clips with Google Veo 3-1
content.value_statement: Short video clips limit creative storytelling and make it hard to build narrative sequences. This skill extends existing Veo clips with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity using the RunComfy CLI and Google Veo 3-1 extend-video API.
actual_capabilities: Extend existing Veo video clips with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity
Chain multiple extend calls to build longer narratives shot-by-shot from a single seed clip
Route between standard and fast extend endpoints based on quality versus cost requirements
Accept a source video URL and natural language continuation prompt for each extend call
Download extended clips to a local output directory using the runcomfy CLI
limitations: Cannot extend videos from sources other than Google Veo 3-1 generated clips
Identity drift accumulates across chained extends beyond 3 to 4 consecutive calls
Requires RunComfy CLI to be installed and authenticated with a valid account
Extend quality depends on source video quality and prompt specificity

use_cases.title: Extend a short video clip to double its length
use_cases.description: Take an existing 8-second Veo clip and extend it to 16 seconds with a natural language prompt describing what should happen next.

use_cases.title: Build a narrative sequence from a single seed clip
use_cases.description: Chain multiple extend calls to create a shot-by-shot narrative, with each call describing one story beat that continues from the previous output.

use_cases.title: Iterate on video compositions with cost control
use_cases.description: Use fast extend for quick draft iterations and switch to the full quality extend endpoint for the final delivery.
use_cases.target_user: AI media researcher or iterative designer

prompt_templates.title: Basic single extend
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video clip by describing what you want to happen next. Keep the description focused on one main action. Use this format: runcomfy run google-deepmind/veo-3-1/extend-video --input '{"video_url": "<source_url>", "prompt": "<what happens next>"}' --output-dir ./out
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting a simple video extension

prompt_templates.title: Camera movement control
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the video with specific camera direction. Include camera instructions in your prompt like 'camera continues pushing in slowly' or 'camera stays static' to prevent drift. Example: runcomfy run google-deepmind/veo-3-1/extend-video --input '{"video_url": "<source_url>", "prompt": "Camera continues pushing in. Character looks down at the object."}' --output-dir ./out
prompt_templates.scenario: User who wants precise camera control in the extended clip

prompt_templates.title: Multi-beat narrative chaining
prompt_templates.prompt: Chain three extends to build a short narrative. First extend: establishing shot. Feed the output as input to the next extend with a new prompt for a medium close-up. Feed that output to a third extend with a character action. Keep each extend focused on one beat and keep individual extends short (3-5 seconds per call) to reduce identity drift across the chain.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building a multi-shot narrative from one seed clip

prompt_templates.title: Cost-optimized iterative workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fast extend endpoint for the first 2-3 draft iterations to explore composition options. Once the sequence is locked, use the full quality extend endpoint for the final render. Route between endpoints by changing the model slug from google-deepmind/veo-3-1/fast/extend-video to google-deepmind/veo-3-1/extend-video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional user optimizing cost versus quality in production

output_examples.input: Extend my 8-second clip of a person walking down a hallway to 16 seconds. The person should continue walking and then stop at a door.
output_examples.output: A 16-second video clip that seamlessly continues the original footage. The person maintains consistent appearance, lighting, and motion as they walk further down the hallway and stop at a door.

output_examples.input: Create a three-shot narrative from this establishing shot of a city street. First extend: a character enters the frame and walks toward the camera. Second extend: cut to a medium close-up of the character's face. Third extend: the character looks down and smiles.
output: Shot 1: The establishing shot continues as a character walks into frame and approaches the camera with consistent lighting and city background.
Shot 2: A medium close-up of the character's face, maintaining consistent identity and lighting from the previous shot.
Shot 3: The close-up continues as the character looks downward and forms a smile, keeping facial identity stable across the chain.

output_examples.input: I need to iterate on this video composition. Run three fast extend drafts first, then finalize with the full quality extend.
output_examples.output: Three fast draft versions of the extended clip produced quickly for comparison, followed by one high-quality final render using the full Veo 3-1 extend endpoint with the best draft composition.

best_practices: Write one main action per extend call for the best results. Multiple actions in a single call produce lower quality output.
Use the fast extend endpoint for early draft iterations and switch to full quality extend for the final delivery.
Keep individual extends short (3-5 seconds) when chaining multiple extends to reduce identity drift across the sequence.
anti_patterns: Describing the full scene again in the extend prompt instead of only describing what should happen next. The source video already provides identity and context.
Combining multiple story beats into a single extend call. Each extend should describe exactly one action or camera movement.
Using extend on videos generated by models other than Veo 3-1. The extend API is designed for Veo clips and results on other sources are unpredictable.

faq.question: What is video extension?
faq.answer: Video extension adds new frames after an existing clip while keeping consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity across the join point.

faq.question: What models does this skill use?
faq.answer: It routes to Google Veo 3-1 extend-video and fast/extend-video endpoints through the RunComfy platform.

faq.question: Do I need special software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the RunComfy CLI installed and authenticated with a RunComfy account. Install with npm install -g at runcomfy/cli.

faq.question: How long can I extend a video?
faq.answer: Each call extends by approximately 8 seconds. You can chain multiple calls to create longer clips, but identity may drift after 3 to 4 consecutive extends.

faq.question: What is the difference between standard and fast extend?
faq.answer: Standard extend offers higher quality for final delivery. Fast extend is lower cost and faster, suitable for draft iterations and composition exploration.

faq.question: Can I extend videos that were not made by Veo 3-1?
faq.answer: No, the extend endpoint is designed for videos generated by Google Veo 3-1 models. Results on other video sources may be unpredictable.
